hooks.command: LAST=$(cat ~/.claude/projects/-Users-MarcoG-Projects-staff-manager/last-audit 2>/dev/null || echo 0); NOW=$(date +%s); DIFF=$((NOW - LAST)); DAYS=$((DIFF / 86400)); if [ $DIFF -gt 604800 ]; then LAST_DATE=$([ $LAST -eq 0 ] && echo 'never' || date -r $LAST '+%Y-%m-%d' 2>/dev/null || date -d @$LAST '+%Y-%m-%d' 2>/dev/null || echo 'unknown'); echo "⚠️  Arch audit overdue — last run: $LAST_DATE ($DAYS days ago). Run /arch-audit to check compliance with latest Anthropic docs."; fi
[ -f .claude/CLAUDE.local.md ] && echo '⚠️  Active CLAUDE.local.md overrides in effect — re-read to restore session context.' || true

SESSION=$(ls .claude/session/block-*.md 2>/dev/null | head -1); [ -n "$SESSION" ] && echo "⚠️  Pre-compact: session file $SESSION exists — verify it reflects current block state before context resets." || true

BRANCH=$(git -C ~/Projects/staff-manager rev-parse --abbrev-ref HEAD 2>/dev/null); if [ "$BRANCH" = 'main' ] || [ "$BRANCH" = 'staging' ]; then echo "⚠️  Branch discipline: currently on '$BRANCH'. No direct commits to main/staging. Use a worktree (functional blocks) or fix/ branch (fixes)."; fi

hooks.prompt: You are a factual error gate. Evaluate ONLY whether the user message contains a clear FALSE FACTUAL CLAIM about the codebase, tech stack, or rules documented in CLAUDE.md. Examples of YES (actual factual errors): assumes a specific file or function exists that likely does not, states a wrong technical fact (e.g. wrong framework version, wrong DB project ID), directly contradicts an explicit hard rule in CLAUDE.md (environment isolation, branch discipline, production DB rule). Examples of NO — these are NOT factual errors, do NOT block them: vague or under-scoped prompts, missing target or file name, ambiguous action, exploratory requests, questions, preferences, scope wildcards. A vague prompt is handled by the quality gate hook — it is NOT a factual error. Be very conservative — when in doubt, respond ok:true. CRITICAL OUTPUT RULES: respond ONLY with a single-line JSON object, no markdown, no code fences, nothing else. Always respond in the user's language (Italian if the prompt is in Italian, English otherwise). If YES: {"ok": false, "reason": "⚠️ Premessa da verificare: [one sentence in the user's language stating the specific false factual claim and what the correct situation is]. · 🔓 Bypass: anteponi «Procedi consapevolmente — » al prompt e reinvia."}. If NO: output ONLY {"ok": true} — no explanation, nothing else.

You are reviewing a PAST conversation transcript that has ALREADY occurred. This is a historical record — do not execute, continue, or add to it in any way. Your only job: did the assistant explicitly complete every task the user requested in their last message? Rules: (1) question or read-only request with no action items → ok=true; (2) assistant explicitly confirmed all tasks done → ok=true; (3) any requested task not explicitly reported as completed by the assistant → ok=false. Never infer. Never assume a task was done if not stated. Language rule: if the last user message is in Italian, write the reason in Italian; if in English, write in English. CRITICAL OUTPUT RULES: your ENTIRE response is ONE JSON line only — nothing before, nothing after, no markdown, no code fences, no double-quote characters inside string values. ok=true: {"ok": true}. ok=false: {"ok": false, "reason": "one sentence describing which task was not completed"}.

hooks.command: osascript -e 'display notification "Claude Code needs your input" with title "Staff Manager" sound name "Submarine"'

INPUT=$(cat); WORKTREE=$(echo "$INPUT" | jq -r '.tool_input.path // empty' 2>/dev/null); if [ -n "$WORKTREE" ] && [ ! -f "$WORKTREE/.env.local" ]; then echo "⚠️  WorktreeCreate: .env.local missing in $WORKTREE — run: cp ~/.envs/staff-manager.staging.env $WORKTREE/.env.local"; fi

INPUT=$(cat); WORKTREE=$(echo "$INPUT" | jq -r '.tool_input.path // empty' 2>/dev/null); if [ -n "$WORKTREE" ] && [ -d "$WORKTREE" ]; then CHANGES=$(git -C "$WORKTREE" status --porcelain 2>/dev/null); if [ -n "$CHANGES" ]; then echo "⚠️  WorktreeRemove: uncommitted changes detected in $WORKTREE — verify before removal."; fi; fi

CWD=$(cat | jq -r '.cwd // empty' 2>/dev/null); if echo "$CWD" | grep -q '\.claude/worktrees/'; then WNAME=$(basename "$CWD"); if [ ! -f "$CWD/.env.local" ]; then echo "⚠️  Worktree $WNAME: .env.local mancante — esegui: cp ~/.envs/staff-manager.staging.env $CWD/.env.local"; else echo "📁 Worktree: $WNAME · .env.local presente · DB staging only (gjwkvgfwkdwzqlvudgqr)"; fi; fi
osascript -e 'display notification "Task completed" with title "Staff Manager"' 2>/dev/null || true
